summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened Impact Church's historic Resurrection Sunday service at a baseball stadium, celebrating under rainy skies. He used the metaphor of a batting average to illustrate humanity's spiritual condition, stating that without Jesus, our spiritual batting average is zero because we all fall short of God's glory. The core message centered on the resurrection of Jesus Christ as the solution, emphasizing that Jesus 'batted a thousand' as our perfect substitute. Pastor Travis highlighted three key truths: God loves us unconditionally, God forgives us completely, and God desires a personal relationship with us, not a religious checklist. He powerfully testified to God's resurrection power in his own life, sharing his recovery from a severe brain aneurysm and stroke that doctors said would leave him dead or in a vegetative state. He challenged the congregation to stop 'looking for the living among the dead' in worldly pursuits and to find true life, purpose, and peace in a relationship with Jesus. The sermon culminated in a call for salvation, inviting those present to stand and pray to receive Christ, resulting in a powerful moment of commitment.
mainPoints: Without Jesus, our spiritual 'batting average' is zero because we all fall short, but Jesus lived perfectly and paid the price for our sins.
God's love is unconditional, His forgiveness is complete, and He is a God of resurrection power who brings dead things back to life.
God desires a personal relationship with us, not religious ritual, and this relationship is the only thing that fills the God-shaped hole in our soul.

versesMentioned.text: for all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God
But God demonstrates his own love for us in this: While we were still sinners, Christ died for us.
Greater love has no one than this: to lay down one’s life for one’s friends.
For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life.

On the first day of the week, very early in the morning, the women took the spices they had prepared and went to the tomb. They found the stone rolled away from the tomb, but when they entered, they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus. While they were wondering about this, suddenly two men in clothes that gleamed like lightning stood beside them. In their fright the women bowed down with their faces to the ground, but the men said to them, "Why do you look for the living among the dead?"
Jesus said to her, "I am the resurrection and the life. The one who believes in me will live, even though they die; and whoever lives by believing in me will never die. Do you believe this?"

Monday.scripture: Romans 3:23 - 'for all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God'
Monday.action: Honestly reflect on one area where you've 'fallen short' this week. Bring it to God without shame.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you that my standing with you doesn't depend on my perfect performance, but on Christ's. Help me to rest in your grace today.

Tuesday.scripture: Romans 5:8 - 'But God demonstrates his own love for us in this: While we were still sinners, Christ died for us.'
Tuesday.action: Write down the phrase 'God loves me anyway' and place it where you'll see it. Let this truth challenge any feeling of being unlovable.
Tuesday.prayer: Father, I am amazed that you loved me at my worst. Help me to receive your love fully and stop trying to earn it.

Wednesday.scripture: John 3:16 - 'For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life.'
Wednesday.action: If you have a relationship with Jesus, thank Him specifically for your salvation. If you're exploring, write down one honest question you have about faith.
Wednesday.prayer: Jesus, thank you for the ultimate gift of yourself. I believe; help my unbelief. Anchor my heart in the reality of eternal life with you.

Thursday.scripture: Luke 24:5 - 'Why do you look for the living among the dead?'
Thursday.focus: Seeking life in the right place.
Thursday.action: Identify one thing you are looking to for life, joy, or purpose (e.g., success, a relationship, money). Consciously choose to turn from it and seek Jesus for that need today.
Thursday.prayer: God, forgive me for looking for life in dead places. You are the resurrection and the life. I turn to you today as my source.

Friday.scripture: John 11:25 - 'Jesus said to her, "I am the resurrection and the life."'
Friday.action: Identify one situation, dream, or relationship in your life that feels 'dead.' In prayer, symbolically place it in Jesus' hands, trusting in His power to restore.
Friday.prayer: Lord of resurrection, nothing is beyond your power to restore. I bring my 'dead' thing to you. I trust you with it. Breathe your life into it.

Saturday.scripture: John 15:13 - 'Greater love has no one than this: to lay down one’s life for one’s friends.'
Saturday.action: Spend 10 minutes in quiet, not asking God for anything, but simply thanking Him for His sacrifice and enjoying His presence. Practice just 'being' with Him.
Saturday.prayer: Jesus, you are my friend and savior. I don't just want to know about you; I want to know you. Thank you for making this relationship possible. I love you.
